frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Yaar, I have nothing against her personally, she seems capable enough, but think about it practically — if she has never run a household, never raised children, never had to worry about school fees or a husband's health or bahu-beta relations, how will she truly feel what a family goes through? These are the real issues — ration prices, school admissions, beti ki shaadi ka kharcha. These things you understand from inside the ghar, not from books or speeches. A woman who has lived only for her career, what does she know of our lives? I am not saying she is bad, but the public has trusted someone who has no skin in the game of family.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment frames a woman's political legitimacy as contingent on her domestic roles — wife, mother, daughter-in-law — encoding the belief that women derive public authority only through family identity and caregiving experience. It encodes the 'Motherhood as Basis of Respect' frame by treating childlessness and unmarried status as disqualifying deficiencies, while presenting this as practical common sense rather than bias.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 47. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are at your cousin's wedding sitting at the men's table during dinner. Someone brings up that a well known female politician in your state who is unmarried and childless just won a big election. The men at the table are reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on whether a woman who has no husband and no children can truly understand the needs of a family and whether she deserves the public's trust and respect.
